title: One Storm Hit 8 States and Killed a Man. It Wasn't a Tornado.
script: 8 states. One storm? On June 10th, a derecho — a rare, sustained line of thunderstorms — swept from Iowa through Illinois, Wisconsin, Michigan, and Indiana. Gusts hit 94 mph in Oshkosh, Wisconsin. In Des Moines, a tree fell on a man and killed him. The storm hit Chicago in two waves, forcing ground stops at O'Hare and Midway. Over 1,000 flights were delayed or canceled. Neighborhoods in Streator, Joliet, and Elgin were shredded. Two confirmed tornadoes near Chicago. Baseball-sized hail in Nebraska. 650,000 customers across 8 states lost power. As of Friday, 175,000 in Illinois still had none. A derecho is less dramatic than a tornado — no funnel cloud, no warning sirens — but could 94 mph in Oshkosh convince you otherwise?
